  • [ - ] PunterBloke - member since 01-Dec-24

    What's gone wrong with Manchester United since Sir Alex Ferguson retired (2012/13 season). After a decade of hiring new managers, nothing has changed yet. David Moyes,Louis van Gaal,Jose Mourinho,Ole Gunnar Solskjaer,Ralf Rangnick,Erik ten Hag, and now Ruben Amorim. Who’s more to blame for bad results? The manager or the players? Or even the board, who lack a vision for Manchester future. I'd say without blinking an eye,the latter to blame. Money talks.

    1 year ago
     +2 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] RichieRochdale - member since 17-Mar-16

    @PunterBloke The root of the problem is the Glazers but, money considerations aside, the practical issue that needs to be solved is ye issue of Player Power. Did you see Rashford on the bench, tonight? The way he portrayed himself; pure arrogance. "Why am I, the mighty Marcus Rashford, sitting on the bench !?!" He should be sprinting up and down the sidelines, foaming at the mouth, dong cartwheels to convince Amorim to put him on! And this terrible, terrible Player Power mentality has continued- probably channelled through the Academy- since Ferguson left.

    1 year ago
